use_cases.title: SA bank migrates 600+ contact-centre agents to AWS Amazon Connect in 5 days
use_cases.problem: Capitec needed to modernise its contact centre, lift the load off legacy on-premises telephony, and improve customer-experience SLAs without a multi-quarter cutover.
use_cases.intervention: Lift-and-shift to AWS Amazon Connect across two critical contact centres, with parallel-run validation and a 5-day cutover.
use_cases.outcome: 600+ agents migrated in 5 days; on-premises dependency retired; cloud-native scalability for peak campaigns.

use_cases.title: SA bank moves 15 years of business-critical data to AWS enterprise data lake
use_cases.problem: Capitec's on-premise data warehouse was reaching end-of-life and lived far from the compute workloads now running in cloud, creating latency and cost overhead.
use_cases.intervention: Migrate 15 years of business-critical data to AWS, implement an enterprise data lake (EDL) as a central repository, and re-anchor downstream analytics on the cloud-native estate.
use_cases.outcome: EDL operational; on-prem data centre retired; analytical capacity scales without capacity planning. Capitec now runs 1.5 trillion reports/month with 27 PB of analytical data.

use_cases.title: Greenfield SOC 2-ready SaaS platform on AWS with IaC + Kubernetes + OTel from day one
use_cases.problem: An INT SaaS scaleup needed a SOC 2 Type II-ready platform within its first six months — without bolting controls on later, and without the multi-month audit-prep cycle most teams hit.
use_cases.intervention: Greenfield AWS landing zone with Terraform-managed accounts, EKS for workloads, ArgoCD for GitOps, OpenTelemetry for traces/metrics/logs, external-secrets-operator for secrets, automated evidence collection for the audit.
use_cases.outcome: SOC 2 Type II observation window started in week 4; audit-prep timeline compressed from typical 3–6 months to ~8 weeks. 60%+ of enterprise prospects now require SOC 2 to engage.

use_cases.title: SA mid-market e-commerce platform cuts infra cost ~30% via right-sizing + reserved capacity + pod density
use_cases.problem: A mid-market retailer's AWS bill grew faster than revenue, with peak-sized instances running 24/7, no reserved-instance coverage, and Kubernetes nodes at 25% utilisation.
use_cases.intervention: FinOps engagement — right-size instances, buy reserved capacity for steady-state, schedule non-prod environments, increase pod density on EKS via resource requests/limits cleanup, kill orphaned resources.
use_cases.outcome: Infrastructure cost reduced ~30% within one billing cycle (Flexera benchmark: 15–25% achievable; peer cases hit 30%). Spend predictability restored.

use_cases.title: Multi-region active-active deployment for INT SaaS across EU + US
use_cases.problem: An INT SaaS expanded into EU enterprise accounts and hit two hard constraints: GDPR data residency for EU customers, and a single-region single-point-of-failure that would not survive due diligence.
use_cases.intervention: Terraform-managed active-active across eu-west-1 + us-east-1, Aurora Global Database for state, Route 53 latency-based routing, blue/green via ArgoCD Rollouts, quarterly chaos failover drills with documented RPO/RTO.
use_cases.outcome: EU data resident in EU; failover drill RPO <5 minutes, RTO <30 minutes; single-region downtime no longer a tenant-impacting event.

use_cases.title: Cyber-resilience uplift for SA insurer to meet Joint Standard 2 of 2024 (effective 1 June 2025)
use_cases.problem: An SA insurer needed to demonstrate readiness for Joint Standard 2 of 2024: MFA on critical systems, quarterly resilience testing including third-party cloud vendors, 24-hour material-incident reporting, and an encrypted-backup strategy.
use_cases.intervention: Inventory of critical systems; MFA roll-out via SSO / WebAuthn; quarterly DR test runbook covering cloud-provider failover; incident-management workflow piped to FSCA reporting template; encrypted-backup audit (KMS keys, retention, restore drill).
use_cases.outcome: Joint Standard 2 of 2024 readiness file delivered before 1 June 2025 commencement. Quarterly DR drill in place. Material-incident reporting tested end-to-end.

use_cases.title: Observability rebuild for SA mid-market retailer — alert volume cut 70%
use_cases.problem: An SA retailer's on-call engineers received 50+ alerts per shift, ~5% of which were actionable. Engineer burnout was eroding retention; MTTR averaged 4+ hours; root causes were being missed in the noise.
use_cases.intervention: Define SLIs + SLOs per service; rewrite paging to burn-rate alerts on error budgets; instrument with OpenTelemetry; consolidate logs + metrics + traces; runbook every page; deprecate 60+ legacy Nagios alerts.
use_cases.outcome: Alert volume reduced 70%; actionable rate up from ~5% to 35%+ (Google SRE benchmark target); MTTR reduced from 4 hours to under 1.

use_cases.title: INT fintech SOC 2 Type II audit prep — IaC-managed controls, 6-week prep vs typical 3-6 months
use_cases.problem: An INT fintech needed SOC 2 Type II to land its next two enterprise deals. Internal estimate was 4–6 months of audit prep with no in-house compliance specialist.
use_cases.intervention: Treat controls as code: Terraform modules enforce logging, encryption, MFA, retention. Automated evidence collection pipes to the auditor. Policy-as-code (OPA / Kyverno) gates merges. Quarterly access review automated.
use_cases.outcome: Type II observation window opened in week 6; auditor confirmed evidence completeness; audit completed 8 weeks after window closed.

use_cases.title: SMB outgrows shared hosting — managed Kubernetes with FinOps guardrails
use_cases.problem: An SMB SaaS founder outgrew shared hosting (uptime, performance, deploy friction) but feared an unbounded cloud bill if they moved to AWS without a controller in the middle.
use_cases.intervention: Move to managed Kubernetes (EKS) with a single-cluster multi-tenant pattern. Hard cloud-budget alerts at 60/80/100% of monthly cap. Karpenter for auto-scaling that scales back down. No reserved instances initially — pay-as-you-go until usage settles.
use_cases.outcome: Cloud bill predictable from month one; deploy frequency lifted from twice-a-month to daily; founder can sleep through the night.
